rationale: The cited paper is mentioned explicitly in the Discussion to provide a specific interpretive lens: asymmetries inferred from elasticities can be driven by market-share differences. The citing paper then uses that point to assess its own asymmetry findings and argues its effects are not attributable to share gaps because private-label shares are comparable to leading national brands. This is more than a passing background citation because it directly informs how the citing paper frames and validates its substantive interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Sethuraman and Srinivasan (2002)'s asymmetric-share-effect insight as a diagnostic benchmark for interpreting its own asymmetry results. It uses the cited paper's argument about share-driven elasticity asymmetry to rule out that explanation in its own context.
